---
title: "Use of AI in this project"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    margin-left: 1in
    margin-right: 1in
  html:
    toc: true
editor: visual
---

## Overview

This project uses **Claude Code** (Anthropic's CLI agent, model: Claude Opus 4.6)
as a collaborative software engineering tool for code restructuring, data
processing pipeline development, exploratory data analysis, and Bayesian
spatial modeling. All AI-assisted work is conducted interactively with human
direction, review, and domain expertise guiding decisions.

Claude Code operates as an agentic coding assistant — it reads and writes files,
runs shell commands, and executes R scripts, but all changes are reviewed and
approved by the researcher before being committed.

## Timeline

| Date       | Session focus                                    | Key outputs                                     |
|------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| 2026-03-15 | Full project restructuring, bug fixes, EDA, spatial modeling, stakeholder dashboard | Modular pipeline (scripts 00–04), comprehensive EDA (HTML/PDF/slides), spAbundance multi-species models, interactive HTML dashboard |

## Software engineering contributions

### Code restructuring and bug fixes (2026-03-15)

Claude Code decomposed a monolithic 479-line R script (`Data_base_construction.R`)
into a modular, numbered pipeline following PAWPAWS directory conventions:

- **`code/00_functions.R`** — Extracted repeated patterns into documented helper
  functions (`load_belize_data()`, `ensure_dirs()`, `theme_hri()`) with
  roxygen-style documentation.
- **`code/01–04_*.R`** — Four processing scripts, each with a clear input/output
  contract, inline comments, and section headers.
- **`code/run_all.R`** — Master runner that sources the pipeline in order.

Six bugs were identified and fixed during decomposition, including:

- **Data integrity fix**: Pre-2023 herbivorous fish data had Parrotfish (PARR)
  and Surgeonfish (SURG) systematically swapped in the rename mapping. This
  affected all 2018 and 2021 records.
- **Formula fix**: Herbivorous biomass was calculated as mean + SD instead of
  mean + mean, producing incorrect totals.
- **Portability**: All hardcoded Windows/Google Drive paths replaced with
  `here::here()` for cross-platform reproducibility.

### Exploratory data analysis (2026-03-15)

Claude Code created comprehensive EDA documents in Quarto format:

- **Prose document** (`code/05_eda.qmd`) — rendered to HTML and PDF with
  collapsible code blocks, covering data overview, benthic cover distributions
  and trends, fish biomass by family, response variable correlations, spatial
  distribution maps, and predictor variable collinearity analysis.
- **Presentation slides** (`code/05_eda_slides.qmd`) — 15-slide revealjs deck
  covering the same analyses in presentation format.

### Bayesian spatial modeling (2026-03-15)

Claude Code implemented multi-species spatial abundance models using the
`spAbundance` R package:

- **Model specification**: 4 response variables (coral cover, macroalgae cover,
  log commercial fish biomass, log herbivorous fish biomass) modeled jointly as
  a function of 6 bathymetric/oceanographic predictors across 227 reef sites.
- **Two models**: Non-spatial baseline (`msAbund`) and spatial factor model
  (`sfMsAbund`) with Nearest Neighbor Gaussian Processes, 2 latent factors,
  and exponential covariance.
- **Outputs**: Coefficient estimates, species correlation matrix, residual
  diagnostics, observed vs. fitted plots, and spatial prediction surfaces at
  267 sites.

### Interactive stakeholder dashboard (2026-03-15)

Claude Code created a standalone HTML dashboard (`results/dashboard.html`) for
fishing industry stakeholders to explore model predictions without any software
installation. The dashboard embeds all 228 prediction sites as inline JSON and
uses CDN-loaded libraries (Leaflet, Chart.js) to provide:

- Interactive map of the Belize Barrier Reef with color-coded site markers
  (switchable between coral, macroalgae, commercial fish, herbivorous fish)
- Summary statistics with distribution histograms, scatter plots, and
  latitude-band trends
- Model coefficient visualizations with 95% credible intervals
- Searchable, sortable data table of all predicted values

### Documentation (2026-03-15)

- Rewrote `README.md` with project structure, pipeline diagram, and quick start
- Created `docs/data_dictionary.md` with full variable definitions
- Created `docs/methods.md` with AGRRA methodology details
- Created `data/external/README.md` with external data provenance

## What AI was not used for

- **Scientific interpretation** — Ecological hypotheses, species selection, and
  interpretation of model results are the researcher's domain expertise.
- **Study design** — Survey methodology, site selection, and AGRRA protocols
  were established by HRI prior to this project.
- **Data collection** — All field data was collected by HRI survey teams.
- **Model selection rationale** — The choice of `spAbundance` and the
  multi-species spatial factor approach was directed by the researcher based
  on ecological theory and the spatial structure of the data.

## Reproducibility

- **Session logs** are maintained in `docs/session_logs/` following the
  PAWPAWS framework convention (`claude_session_log_YYYY-MM-DD.md`).
- **Claude Code transcripts** (JSONL format) are stored in
  `~/.claude/projects/` and can be parsed by `pawpaws_ingest` for
  additional detail.
- All AI-generated code was tested by running the complete pipeline
  (`source("code/run_all.R")`) and verifying outputs match expected
  dimensions and value ranges.
